msgByRace.goblin: You jam the twig into a crack in the rock and twist. It holds. Somehow you made a hatchet.
msgByRace.human: You bind the twig to the rock with careful pressure. A rough hatchet takes shape.
msgByRace.elf: The materials resist at first, then yield. The result is crude but true.

msgByRace.goblin: You lash the twig across the branch in a cross. It's ugly. It might stop a hit.
msgByRace.human: You bind the twig around the branch into a rough frame. A crude shield.
msgByRace.elf: You weave the twig through the grain of the branch. It holds better than it should.

msgByRace.goblin: You lash the twig across the branch in a cross. It's ugly. It might stop a hit.
msgByRace.human: You bind the twig around the branch into a rough frame. A crude shield.
msgByRace.elf: You weave the twig through the grain of the branch. It holds better than it should.

msgByRace.goblin: You wedge the rock onto the branch end and bite down on it to test. Sharp enough. Good enough.
msgByRace.human: You split the branch tip and seat the rock inside it, binding it firm. A spear.
msgByRace.elf: Stone and wood, joined. You feel something ancient about this. You made it anyway.

msgByRace.goblin: You wedge the rock onto the branch end and bite down on it to test. Sharp enough. Good enough.
msgByRace.human: You split the branch tip and seat the rock inside it, binding it firm. A spear.
msgByRace.elf: Stone and wood, joined. You feel something ancient about this. You made it anyway.

msgByRace.goblin: You press the coin into the wood. It catches the light. You feel powerful and stupid at the same time.
msgByRace.human: You embed the coin in the face of the shield. Worthless metal, but it gleams.
msgByRace.elf: A false thing pressed into a crude thing. And yet — it is beautiful. You hate that.
